mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 500K persons signals robust labor market recovery and consumer income expansion. This drives consumption and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth (Spearman rho < 0.15 over any 2-year rolling window), or returns are negative in > 50% of months with PAYEMS growth > 500K.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, forward equity returns are positive over 20 days due to earnings upgrade expectations and reduced recession risk. The mechanism requires both signals to be active.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both positive MoM growth
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 444,926M and HOUST at 1,135K as of 2020-11-15
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression for cyclical firms. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 2% creates a negative real rate environment that erodes equity risk premiums and signals future monetary tightening. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate policy normalization.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 235.982 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2020-11-15
regime_hint: inflation acceleration with accommodative policy lag
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when inflation exceeds 3% YoY and FEDFUNDS < 2%, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions and increased recession risk. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums widen.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp MoM, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating quarter-over-quarter above 2% annualized rate signals robust economic expansion and earnings growth. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors upgrade profit forecasts and reduce recession probability.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth (Spearman rho < 0.12 over any 3-year rolling window), or returns are negative in > 50% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both declining simultaneously signal broad-based economic weakness affecting both supply capacity and labor demand. This joint deterioration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% MoM AND PAYEMS declines > 100K MoM
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.1018 and PAYEMS at 141,679K as of 2020-11-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.
